toolCalls.name: edit

data.content: Now replace all `DateTime.UtcNow` calls with `_timeProvider.GetUtcNow()`. Note: `GetUtcNow()` returns `DateTimeOffset`, so I'll use `.UtcDateTime` where a `DateTime` is needed.

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"old_str": "            CreatedAt = DateTime.UtcNow,\n            ExpiresAt = DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(validDays)", "new_str": "            CreatedAt = _timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime,\n            ExpiresAt = _timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime.AddDays(validDays)", "path": "/tmp/sv-d95d9d6c28a84851b3e70d3228cd58ac/fixtures/ready-to-migrate/Services/CouponService.cs"}

data.content:
Done. Here's what changed in `CouponService.cs`:

1. **Added `TimeProvider` field** and constructor parameter (DI will inject it)
2. **Replaced all 4 `DateTime.UtcNow` calls** with `_timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime` — in `CreateCoupon`, `IsValid`, and `TimeUntilExpiry`

`AuditLogger.cs` is untouched. Since `TimeProvider.System` is already registered in your DI container (`Program.cs`), no other changes are needed. In tests you can now use `Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider` to control time.
